title: Signature validation of ASCII armored OpenPGP signatures fail
body:
When an atomic signature is ASCII armor encoded, validation of it fails due to not being able to determine the format. 

skopeo 1.21.0
```
-> skopeo --debug copy docker://stagex/core-bash:5.2.37 dir:sx-core-bash
FATA[0002] copying system image from manifest list: Source image rejected: parsing signature https://sigs.stagex.tools/stagex/core-bash@sha256=5b598c14eef61148baf3f5a2830a214a5985b5d3544b019e3d0ed53c6b66989a/signature-2: unrecognized signature format, starting with binary 0x2d 
```

Which comes from [signature.go FromBlob](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/38ab7bf87d7a20e1a7ecc27193cf17d51c806f9e/image/internal/signature/signature.go#L84). 

```
sq packet dump signatures/stagex/core-bash@sha256=5b598c14eef61148baf3f5a2830a214a5985b5d3544b019e3d0ed53c6b66989a/signature-2
Compressed Data Packet, old CTB, indeterminate length
│   Algorithm: ZIP
│
├── One-Pass Signature Packet, old CTB, 13 bytes
│       Version: 3
│       Type: Binary
│       Pk algo: EdDSA
│       Hash algo: SHA512
│       Issuer: DD9F5D50CAA0BAD4
│       Last: true
│
├── Literal Data Packet, new CTB, 237 bytes
│       Format: Binary data
│       Timestamp: 2026-01-29 02:39:57 UTC
│       Content: {"critical":{"identity":{"docker-referen...
│
└── Signature Packet, old CTB, 117 bytes
        Version: 4
        Type: Binary
        Pk algo: EdDSA
        Hash algo: SHA512
        Hashed area:
          Issuer Fingerprint: 2093E332AE21416C536355C0DD9F5D50CAA0BAD4
          Signature creation time: 2026-01-29 02:39:57 UTC
        Unhashed area:
          Issuer: DD9F5D50CAA0BAD4
        Digest prefix: 1F84
        Level: 0 (signature over data)
```

User had ascii armor enabled by default
```
# gpg.conf
armor
```

We generate our signatures using [gnupg directly](https://codeberg.org/stagex/stagex/src/branch/main/src/sign.sh#L149), we can change our script to include `--no-armor` which will take precedence over the users config, but I imagine we won't be the last to come across this.

We have dearmored the signatures on our lookaside server, but here was the previous [ASCII armored](https://codeberg.org/stagex/signatures/src/commit/131fba287cc3fed0890a9fc45006534c29603a37/stagex/core-bash@sha256=5b598c14eef61148baf3f5a2830a214a5985b5d3544b019e3d0ed53c6b66989a/signature-2) one

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I think the most pedantic reading of https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/38ab7bf87d7a20e1a7ecc27193cf17d51c806f9e/image/docs/containers-signature.5.md#openpgp-signature-verification is that an armored message is not expected, but the spec does not say so directly.

The code you pointed at was “only” added in 2022; before, it’s possible that the GPG backend was accepting armored signatures — but the OpenPGP backend _never_ accepted armored signatures since it was added in 2017.

So I think it makes more sense to explicitly document ASCII-armored signatures to be invalid. (Yes, there is #307, but that’s explicitly violating the spec for other reasons anyway … and the linked code never came up; so it’s possible that this just never worked other than maybe a _very_ special caller.)

Care to write a PR that clarifies the documentation?

@mtrmac pointed me here from https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/307.

We also use armored signatures (in our case inline). The way we pass through this is by prefixing with `\x00simple-signing` as the `signature.go` code hints at:

https://github.com/coreos/coreos-assembler/blob/4053c2f8c5c61c0ad0ce2c5199553e1c152ac0a0/src/cmd-sign#L383

We're fully aware we're technically colouring outside the lines here. We don't currently have another viable signing option but will move off of it once we do. (All to say, only do this too if you accept the responsibility!)
